samples.xml_description:
Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. Clock. Reset. 1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0). 8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division. 8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division. 1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present. 1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged. 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready. 16-bit output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits. 0 1 2

Initialize the divider:

  • When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.
  • The shift register (SR) is initialized with the absolute value of the dividend, shifted left by one bit.
  • NEG_DIVISOR is set to the negated absolute value of the divisor.
  • The counter (cnt) is initialized to 1 and start_cnt is set to 1 to begin the division process.

Division process:

  • When start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps:
  • When cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete.
  • cnt and start_cnt are cleared.
  • The shift register SR is updated with the final remainder and quotient.
  • Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result:
  • The subtraction of NEG_DIVISOR is computed.
  • The carry-out is determined using a multiplexer.
  • The shift register SR is updated by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.

Result validity:

  • res_valid is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed.
  • res_valid is set to zero if rst is high.
  • When cnt reaches 8, res_ready is set to 1.
  • Otherwise, if res_valid and res_ready is all high, res_valid is set to 0.
